markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Choose direct load or exact lookup before graph/hybrid retrieval when IDs or authoritative paths are known. This MT belongs to RetrievalContextAndRanking. Retrieve project knowledge through explainable direct, graph, hybrid, and passage fallback modes.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Retrieval must choose explicit modes: none, direct_load, exact_lookup, graph_traversal, hybrid_rag, or passage_fallback, with non_hybrid_reason where applicable.
Context bundles must be bounded, cited, explainable, restartable, and inspectable through backend and visual-debug surfaces.
scope.expected_behavior: CheapestAuthoritativePathPlanner leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: CheapestAuthoritativePathPlanner is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

evidence: Built+verified bc2aaa63; RetrievalContextAndRanking: 46 unit + 10 real-PG tests pass; cheapest-authoritative planner records mode+skip-reason in replayable RetrievalTrace; reuses MT-060 trace/bundle substrate (1 new migration 0260); MT-129 additive QueryRetrievalMode extension; folded-stub bridges (139-142) preserve ProjectBrain/SemanticCatalog/AIReady/ContextPack intent as backend contracts.

evidence.finding: WP/MT/relationship handles never confirmed against PG (unsatisfiable plan, no degrade); only entity/source were checked
evidence.fix: Every handle kind existence-checked by real PG read (WP/MT vs indexed work_packet/micro_task entities, relationship vs knowledge_edges.relationship_id); dangling handles degrade to a broader mode and are recorded on PlannedRetrieval.dangling_handles with actor-visible reason()

findings.title: dangling-handle degrade holds only for entity/source handles; WP/MT/relationship handles never confirm
findings.evidence: planner.rs first_confirmed_handle: EntityId/EntityIdentity (266-285) and SourceId (298-305) do a real PG read, but WorkPacketId (286-291), MicroTaskId (292-297), and RelationshipId (306-311) return Some unconditionally. A fabricated WP-9999 / MT-9999 / REL-fake yields a confident direct_load/exact_lookup plan for a nonexistent record with no degrade and no recorded unconfirmed reason. The dangling-handle test (knowledge_retrieval_pipeline_tests.rs:124) covers only EntityId.
findings.fix: Confirm WP/MT against the packet/MT store and relationship_id against knowledge_edges; degrade (and record the reason) when absent, matching the entity path.

findings.title: freshness_uncertain stamps a reason but never downgrades a stale-but-confirmed entity
findings.evidence: planner.rs:177-183 sets NonHybridReason::FreshnessUncertain yet still returns direct_load for the confirmed handle; no StalenessVerdict is consulted, so a confirmed-but-stale record is served directly (reason recorded, mode unchanged).
findings.fix: Consult a staleness signal and downgrade to a fresher mode (or passage fallback) when the authoritative record is stale, not just record the reason.

claim_mismatches.claim: a dangling handle degrades to a broader mode rather than producing a plan that cannot be satisfied
claim_mismatches.reality: true only for entity/source handles; WP/MT/relationship handles are authoritative-by-construction and produce an unsatisfiable plan with no degrade

missing_tests: test: dangling WorkPacketId / MicroTaskId / RelationshipId degrades to a broader mode (currently produces a confident unsatisfiable plan)

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-130 implements a deterministic CheapestAuthoritativePathPlanner that selects the cheapest authoritative retrieval mode (none/direct_load/exact_lookup/graph_traversal/hybrid_rag) and records the skip reason, exactly as required by current Master Spec 2.3.14 [ADD v02.178]. Every authoritative handle kind is existence-checked via real PostgreSQL reads before anchoring a direct/exact plan; dangling handles degrade to a broader mode and are recorded. Real-PG tests prove the behavior; the prior adversarial MEDIUM is genuinely fixed.
findings: planner.rs:194-315 implements the cheapest-authoritative mode ladder (user-forced -> confirmed-handle direct/exact -> graph_traversal -> policy-blocked -> hybrid_rag), recording NonHybridReason on each non-hybrid path. Matches Master Spec v02.194 02-system-architecture.md:2817 (2.3.14 [ADD v02.178]) verbatim in intent.

planner.rs:351-434 first_confirmed_handle now existence-checks EVERY handle kind via real PG reads: EntityId/EntityIdentity->get_knowledge_entity(_by_identity), WorkPacketId/MicroTaskId->get_knowledge_entity_by_identity(kind=WorkPacket/MicroTask), SourceId->get_knowledge_source, RelationshipId->get_knowledge_edge_by_relationship_id. All are real sqlx SELECTs (storage/knowledge.rs:3944-3975 etc.).

Dangling handles degrade to a broader mode and are recorded on PlannedRetrieval.dangling_handles with an actor-visible reason() (planner.rs:138-177, 416-432) instead of producing an unsatisfiable plan.

Prior adversarial-v2 MEDIUM (WP/MT/relationship handles returned confirmed unconditionally) is genuinely fixed in commit fb71d0f6 and covered by real-PG tests dangling_wp_mt_relationship_handles_degrade_and_record (knowledge_retrieval_pipeline_tests.rs:154-184) and indexed_wp_mt_relationship_handles_confirm (190-286).

Tests run against a real managed PostgreSQL via MemoryFixture/pool_for (knowledge_memory_fixtures.rs:8,25-26,146-149); header explicitly states NO SQLite, NO mocks; tests SKIP loudly when PG binaries absent. No forbidden tech (SQLite/Elasticsearch/Docker) present.
Sub-rule 1 clean: only a doc-comment reference to a folded governance 'stub' (planner.rs:21), no reachable todo!/unimplemented!/Unavailable escape.

Open LOW (non-blocking): freshness_uncertain (planner.rs:226-232) stamps NonHybridReason::FreshnessUncertain but does not downgrade a confirmed-but-stale record; this is a refinement, not a contradiction of the MT or spec.

Aside (non-blocking, expected): impl lives in knowledge_retrieval/planner.rs, not the relevant_files paths listed in the MT (retrieval_mode.rs/context_bundle.rs); paperwork drift only, the real implementation is present and proven.
evidence_checked: grep for deferred-live escapes and forbidden tech

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
